target.name: Cerebras
target.founder: Andrew Feldman, Gary Lauterbach, Michael James, Sean Lie, Jean-Philippe Fricker

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Cerebras is a public-stage company in Chips & Semiconductors that has raised $6.6B+ and currently trades at a $23B valuation. The peer universe consists of 3 public comparables (Credo Semiconductor at $5.0B, Ouster at $1.5B, and Aeva Technologies at $1.0B) plus 8 private frontier-tech peers in the sector.

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a $1.5B implied valuation across the 3 same-sector public comparables, with a range of $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 23.4x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD) cannot be applied due to absence of Cerebras revenue data on file. This single-method triangulation produces a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered at $1.5B.

The substantial gap between the peer-derived range ($1.3B–$1.7B) and Cerebras's current public-market valuation of $23B suggests either significant market-priced premiums tied to growth expectations, differentiated technology positioning, or revenue scale not captured in this comparable set. Key uncertainties include Cerebras's actual revenue run rate and customer concentration, which would enable revenue-multiple validation. The analysis supports a $1.5B central estimate based on peer positioning alone, pending reconciliation with disclosed financial metrics.
